title: Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid
content: UN humanitarians working in war-torn Sudan on Friday highlighted rapidly growing needs among the more than 100,000 displaced people now sheltering in camps in the city of El Obeid.
snippet: UN humanitarians working in war-torn Sudan on Friday highlighted rapidly growing needs among the more than 100,000 displaced people now sheltering in camps in the city of El Obeid.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Looted gold and gum arabic are bankrolling Sudan's war, UN warns
content: Alongside gold, the little-known Sudanese commodity gum arabic – used in soft drinks, food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals – continues to fuel the war.
snippet: Alongside gold, the little-known Sudanese commodity gum arabic – used in soft drinks, food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals – continues to fuel the war.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: New cholera outbreak alert for Sudan’s war-weary communities
content: In war-torn Sudan, a deadly new cholera outbreak has already claimed more than 100 lives, heightening serious concerns for vulnerable communities including in besieged El-Obeid, where daily drone attacks have continued to hamper aid access.
snippet: In war-torn Sudan, a deadly new cholera outbreak has already claimed more than 100 lives, heightening serious concerns for vulnerable communities including in besieged El-Obeid, where daily drone
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Use of explosive weapons ‘must stop’ in eastern DR Congo: Türk
content: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk urged the armed forces of the Democratic Republic of the Congo and the Rwanda-backed M23 armed group to cease hostilities as he deplored recent civilian deaths on Thursday.
snippet: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk urged the armed forces of the Democratic Republic of the Congo and the Rwanda-backed M23 armed group to cease hostilities as he deplored recent
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Sudan’s people subject to ‘relentless’ drone strikes in El Obeid
content: The war in Sudan threatens to spiral further as the strategically-important town of El Obeid continues to face “relentless” drone attacks by advancing paramilitary militias, the UN’s human rights chief, Volker Türk, said on Friday.

sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Racism significantly impacts access to sport: Independent expert
content: From high participation costs and restrictions on Palestinian athletes – to bans on Muslim women wearing hijabs – racism continues to shape who gets to participate in sport, a new UN report published on Thursday has found.
snippet: From high participation costs and restrictions on Palestinian athletes – to bans on Muslim women wearing hijabs – racism continues to shape who gets to participate in sport, a new UN report published
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: World News in Brief: Afghanistan airstrikes, rights violations in DR Congo, Gaza displacement
content: At least 28 civilians were killed and 49 injured in airstrikes carried out in Paktya, Paktika and Kunar provinces in Afghanistan on Sunday evening, the UN Mission in the country has reported.
snippet: At least 28 civilians were killed and 49 injured in airstrikes carried out in Paktya, Paktika and Kunar provinces in Afghanistan on Sunday evening, the UN Mission in the country has reported.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Venezuela quake: ‘People still terrified to re-enter what were their homes’
content: Aid agencies on Friday highlighted massive needs across Venezuela caused by a double earthquake disaster that has killed at least 235 people so far, with search and rescue for people trapped under the rubble still the top priority.
snippet: Aid agencies on Friday highlighted massive needs across Venezuela caused by a double earthquake disaster that has killed at least 235 people so far, with search and rescue for people trapped under the
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Report details widespread use of sexual violence in Sudan war
content: A new UN human rights report issued on Tuesday details the brutality and scale of conflict-related sexual violence in Sudan since war erupted in April 2023 and its profound, long-term impacts on victims, families and communities.
snippet: A new UN human rights report issued on Tuesday details the brutality and scale of conflict-related sexual violence in Sudan since war erupted in April 2023 and its profound, long-term impacts on
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Myanmar: Aid decline compounds suffering amid ongoing military attacks
content: A drop in humanitarian assistance is worsening the suffering of millions of people in Myanmar after five years of conflict-related violence, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Monday.
snippet: A drop in humanitarian assistance is worsening the suffering of millions of people in Myanmar after five years of conflict-related violence, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: AI is getting women wrong as gender bias persists, data reveals
content: As artificial intelligence reshapes how people work, communicate and access information, UN Women warned on Monday that the technology is reproducing old gender stereotypes, which amplify online abuse and leave women out of the decisions that will define the digital future.
snippet: As artificial intelligence reshapes how people work, communicate and access information, UN Women warned on Monday that the technology is reproducing old gender stereotypes, which amplify online abuse
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Old and new challenges for the Human Rights Council as it turns 20
content: It’s 20 years to the day since the UN Human Rights Council began its work as the world’s principal forum tasked with promoting and defending fundamental rights everywhere, particularly the world’s most vulnerable people.
snippet: It’s 20 years to the day since the UN Human Rights Council began its work as the world’s principal forum tasked with promoting and defending fundamental rights everywhere, particularly the world’s
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: From ‘media deserts’ to the invisibility of women, rights experts spotlight latest trends
content: From the invisibility of women and girls to “media deserts” amid an artificial intelligence (AI) tsunami, dozens of UN independent experts have been shedding light on the battle for equal rights worldwide as the Geneva-based Human Rights Council continued its 62nd session on Thursday.
snippet: From the invisibility of women and girls to “media deserts” amid an artificial intelligence (AI) tsunami, dozens of UN independent experts have been shedding light on the battle for equal rights
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Rights defender killings hit record high as UN pushes to shore up humanitarian action
content: Attacks against human right defenders have reached record levels over the past year, according to a new report issued on Wednesday by the UN human rights office, OHCHR.
snippet: Attacks against human right defenders have reached record levels over the past year, according to a new report issued on Wednesday by the UN human rights office, OHCHR.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Hate speech is ‘first step down the path of dehumanisation’
content: As online platforms continue to fuel a surge in real-world violence against vulnerable communities, UN Secretary-General António Guterres is warning that freedom of expression must never be used to justify hate speech.
snippet: As online platforms continue to fuel a surge in real-world violence against vulnerable communities, UN Secretary-General António Guterres is warning that freedom of expression must never be used to
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: UN urges adherence to mine ban treaty
content: Countries must uphold international law limiting the use of anti-personnel mines, which kill and maim civilians long after conflicts have ended, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Tuesday.
snippet: Countries must uphold international law limiting the use of anti-personnel mines, which kill and maim civilians long after conflicts have ended, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts
content: As Sudan’s conflict enters a fourth year, civilians are increasingly trapped not only by frontline violence but by fear, disappearance and detention, according to an update by the Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for the Sudan to the 62nd Session of the UN Human Rights Council in Geneva on Monday.
snippet: As Sudan’s conflict enters a fourth year, civilians are increasingly trapped not only by frontline violence but by fear, disappearance and detention, according to an update by the Independent
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights

title: ‘Grave concern’ after dozens of women arrested in Afghanistan for dress violations
content: Gender equality agency UN Women is “gravely concerned” by the arrest of at least 30 women in Herat city last weekend in Afghanistan for allegedly violating dress requirements imposed by Taliban authorities.
snippet: Gender equality agency UN Women is “gravely concerned” by the arrest of at least 30 women in Herat city last weekend in Afghanistan for allegedly violating dress requirements imposed by Taliban
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Human Rights
